explanation:
**Gardening and growing vegetables** combines agronomy, botany, and climate knowledge. Success depends on matching plants to available sunlight.

## How It's Calculated

Recommendation based on your plant's light requirement category—fast and free. This calculator applies a straightforward formula using your inputs, checking typical ranges and alerting if values seem unreasonable.

## Final Notes

This calculation is for reference only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a professional. Data updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How do I measure sunlight hours in my garden?
faq.a: Observe your garden for one full day. Note when direct sun first hits the area and when it becomes fully shaded. Count the total hours of direct sunlight.

faq.q: What's the difference between full sun, partial sun, and shade?
faq.a: Full sun: 6+ hours of direct sunlight daily. Partial sun: 3-6 hours. Shade: less than 3 hours or dappled light all day.

faq.q: Does direction matter—north vs. south facing?
faq.a: Yes. In the Northern Hemisphere, south-facing areas get more sunlight. In the Southern Hemisphere, north-facing areas receive more sun. Your hemisphere matters.

faq.q: Does filtered or dappled sunlight count?
faq.a: Yes. Light filtering through tree leaves counts as partial sun. A semi-transparent tree canopy provides roughly 50% of direct sunlight.

faq.q: How much light do indoor plants need?
faq.a: A sunny south-facing window in the Northern Hemisphere provides about 4-6 equivalent hours. East/west windows give roughly 2-4 hours.

faq.q: Can LED grow lights replace natural sunlight?
faq.a: Yes. A 20-40W LED grow light can effectively replace direct sunlight for one plant, depending on distance and plant type.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, completely free. No account, registration, or hidden fees. All calculators on Hacé Cuentas are free.
Results are approximate guides based on standard, internationally-validated formulas. For critical decisions, consult with a horticultural professional.
